tossups.question: British composer Tom Lane recorded the sounds of this bridge's cables with contact microphones for a 2014 "river cantata." It was formerly known as the Macken Street Bridge, so named for connecting Macken and Guild Streets. Per the specifications of a 1997 "docklands master plan," this bridge was built to rotate 90 degrees so larger boats could travel down the River (*) Liffey. This bridge lies two miles downstream from the James Joyce Bridge, also designed by Santiago Calatrava. It resembles a harp laid on its side, its 31 cables representing the instrument's strings. For 10 points, identify this Dublin bridge whose namesake wrote an absurdist play in which Vladimir and Estragon await the arrival of the mysterious Godot.
